atmospheric_co2_ppm.label: Atmospheric CO₂ concentration
atmospheric_co2_ppm.source: NOAA (global mean surface CO₂, not Mauna Loa alone)
atmospheric_co2_ppm.note: Global mean; Mauna Loa single-station value differs slightly.

co2_fossil_gt.label: Fossil CO₂ emissions
co2_fossil_gt.note: Fossil fuel CO₂ only. Does not include land-use change or other GHGs.

ghg_total_gtco2e.label: Total GHG emissions
ghg_total_gtco2e.source: OWID (Jones et al. 2025, based on EDGAR v8.0 + GCB)
ghg_total_gtco2e.note: All greenhouse gases (CO₂, CH₄, N₂O, F-gases) as CO₂e using AR6 GWP100 values. EXCLUDES land-use change and forestry (LULUCF). Including LULUCF adds ~4–5 GtCO₂e/yr (UNEP EGR 2024 reports ~59 GtCO₂e with LULUCF). Different sources report different totals due to: LULUCF inclusion, GWP generation (AR5 vs AR6), gas coverage, and inventory methodology (EDGAR vs UNFCCC vs PRIMAP).

renewable_share_electricity_pct.label: Renewable share of electricity generation
renewable_share_electricity_pct.note: Denominator: total electricity generation (~30% globally). NOT share of final energy (~13%).

temperature_anomaly_c.label: Global temperature anomaly
temperature_anomaly_c.note: Baseline: 1850-1900 (IPCC AR6 standard). 2025 value is preliminary (partial-year HadCRUT5 data).

clean_energy_investment_t.label: Clean energy investment
clean_energy_investment_t.note: Includes renewables, nuclear, efficiency, networks, storage, EVs.

deaths_ambient_pm25_m.label: Deaths from all outdoor PM2.5
deaths_ambient_pm25_m.source: IHME Global Burden of Disease 2023 (ambient PM2.5)
deaths_ambient_pm25_m.note: Deaths from ALL sources of outdoor PM2.5 (fossil fuels, dust, wildfires, agriculture, etc.). Fossil fuel combustion accounts for ~1.3M of these (~33%, McDuffie et al. 2021). NOT combined with household air pollution deaths (~2.8M/yr, separate category).

deaths_household_air_m.label: Deaths from household air pollution (solid fuels)
deaths_household_air_m.source: IHME Global Burden of Disease 2023 (household solid fuels)
deaths_household_air_m.note: Household air pollution from solid-fuel cooking. NOT directly addressed by electricity decarbonization. Requires clean cooking access programs (South Asia, Sub-Saharan Africa).

electricity_access_pct.label: Population with electricity access
electricity_access_pct.note: Population-weighted global average (standard World Bank / IEA methodology). Masks large regional disparities.

clean_cooking_access_pct.label: Population with clean cooking fuel access
clean_cooking_access_pct.note: Population-weighted global average (WHO / World Bank methodology). Closely linked to household air pollution mortality.

current_policies_warming_c.label: Projected warming under current policies
current_policies_warming_c.note: 3.1°C = warming if all current policies are fully implemented (UNEP 2024). If countries meet their unconditional NDC pledges: ~2.8°C. With conditional pledges: ~2.6°C. Click for historical temperature trend (HadCRUT5).

renewable_share_total_energy_pct.label: Renewable share of total energy
renewable_share_total_energy_pct.note: Share of renewables in total final energy consumption (not just electricity). Much lower than electricity share (~31%) because transport, heating, and industry still rely heavily on fossil fuels.

subsidies_global_bn.label: Global fossil fuel subsidies (explicit)
subsidies_global_bn.note: Explicit subsidies only (government price support). IMF estimates ~$7T/yr including implicit costs (unpriced pollution, climate damage).

subsidies_implicit_tn.label: Total subsidies incl. implicit (IMF)
subsidies_implicit_tn.note: Includes explicit subsidies plus unpriced externalities (air pollution, climate damage, forgone consumption taxes).

subsidies_top_country.label: Top subsidizer (consumer + production)
subsidies_top_country.source: IISD Fossil Fuel Subsidy Tracker (IEA + OECD data)
subsidies_top_country.note: IEA price-gap consumer subsidies. Rankings vary by methodology and year. For 2023 (IISD/IEA+OECD combined): Russia, Germany, Iran, China, Japan among top subsidizers. IMF broad definition (incl. unpriced externalities) ranks China #1 globally. US shows ~$0 under IEA price-gap but ~$18B under OECD production subsidy accounting.

predictions_solar_ratio.label: Solar: actual vs IEA 2006 forecast
predictions_solar_ratio.source: IEA WEO 2006 Reference Scenario vs IRENA actuals
predictions_solar_ratio.note: IEA WEO 2006 Reference Scenario projected ~142 GW solar by 2030. Actual 2023: 1,415 GW (~10× more). Even later editions (WEO 2010: ~400 GW for 2030) underestimated by ~3.5×. Demonstrates systematic underestimation of solar adoption curves.

predictions_wind_ratio.label: Wind: actual vs IEA 2006 forecast
predictions_wind_ratio.note: Wind forecasts were less dramatically wrong than solar, but still consistently underestimated by 2–3×.

predictions_ccs_shortfall.label: CCS: actual vs IEA WEO 2009 forecast for 2030
predictions_ccs_shortfall.note: IEA WEO 2009 projected 700 MtCO₂/yr CCS by 2030. Actual 2024: ~55 MtCO₂/yr (0.08× of forecast). CCS has consistently underperformed projections.

health_deaths_fossil_pm25.label: Deaths from fossil fuel PM2.5
health_deaths_fossil_pm25.note: Range reflects methodology: ~1.3M (McDuffie, GBD sector attribution), ~5.1M (Lelieveld, atmospheric chemistry), ~8.7M (Vohra, updated CRFs). GBD total from ALL outdoor PM2.5 = ~4.9M. Click for GBD time series.

health_deaths_per_twh_coal.label: Coal: deaths per TWh
health_deaths_per_twh_coal.note: Lifecycle deaths per TWh from coal. ~1,000× more than wind or solar. Includes mining, air pollution, accidents.

health_heat_deaths.label: Climate-attributable heat deaths
health_heat_deaths.note: ~37% of total heat-related deaths (~546K/yr, Lancet Countdown 2025) are attributable to anthropogenic climate change (Vicedo-Cabrera et al. 2021, Nature Climate Change). This yields ~200K/yr climate-attributable heat deaths. Separate from air pollution.

health_disaster_deaths.label: Weather/climate disaster deaths
health_disaster_deaths.note: Deaths from floods, storms, droughts, extreme temperatures, wildfires (3-year avg ~75K/yr). Not all individually attributable to climate change, but climate change increases frequency and severity. Highly variable year-to-year.

health_cumulative_climate_deaths.label: Total climate-attributable deaths
health_cumulative_climate_deaths.note: Sum of fossil fuel PM2.5 (~1.4M, McDuffie 33% of GBD), climate-attributable heat (~200K, 37% of total heat deaths per Vicedo-Cabrera 2021), and weather/climate disaster deaths (~8K). Uses conservative estimates throughout. Higher PM2.5 methods (Lelieveld/Vohra) would yield 5–9M+ total.
